text: I'm going to give him the benefit of the doubt for now.

glossary.word: give someone the benefit of the doubt
glossary.meaning: choose to believe someone is honest/right when you're not 100% sure

text: To be fair, she hasn't really had a chance to explain herself yet.

glossary.meaning: being honest / considering all sides

text: I wouldn't read too much into it if I were you.

glossary.meaning: find meaning that isn't really there

text: That's not what I meant — let me put it differently.
I have a feeling something's off, but I can't quite explain why.

glossary.meaning: something isn't right / feels wrong

text: We've been going back and forth on this for weeks now.

glossary.meaning: switch between options without deciding

text: I'd hate to put you on the spot, but what's your take?

glossary.meaning: force someone to answer immediately
your opinion / interpretation

text: To cut a long story short, we ended up cancelling the trip.

glossary.word: to cut a long story short
glossary.meaning: to summarise briefly / skip the details

text: It looks like we'll have to make do with what we've got.

glossary.meaning: manage with what's available

text: I've been keeping an eye on the situation, just in case.

glossary.meaning: watch carefully / monitor

text: Now that you mention it, I do remember something about that.
I don't want to jump the gun, but it's looking promising.

glossary.meaning: act too soon / before the right time

text: Let's not get ahead of ourselves — there's still a lot to do.

glossary.meaning: celebrate or plan too early

text: I'm willing to give it a shot if you are.

glossary.meaning: try / attempt

text: To be honest, I'm not sure that's a hill worth dying on.

glossary.meaning: an issue important enough to fight hard for

text: I'd rather not get involved in this if I can help it.

glossary.meaning: if I can avoid it

text: Could we possibly push the meeting back a bit?

glossary.meaning: postpone / reschedule for later

text: I'd hate to leave you hanging, so let me know either way.

glossary.meaning: fail to give a needed response / keep someone waiting

text: You'd think they would have figured it out by now.

glossary.meaning: one would expect (used with mild frustration)

text: I'll admit, I didn't see that one coming.

glossary.meaning: anticipate / predict it

text: Looking on the bright side, at least we learned something.

glossary.meaning: focus on the positive

text: I'm trying to figure out where things went wrong.

glossary.meaning: work out / understand

text: The way I see it, we have two reasonable options.
It's a tough call, but I'd lean towards the safer one.

glossary.meaning: a difficult decision

text: Sometimes you've just got to roll with the punches.

glossary.meaning: adapt to whatever happens / accept difficulties calmly

text: I don't want to come off as ungrateful, but I expected more.

glossary.meaning: appear to be / give the impression of

text: That's water under the bridge as far as I'm concerned.

glossary.meaning: a past problem that doesn't matter anymore

text: I wouldn't lose any sleep over it if I were you.

glossary.meaning: worry about

text: They've been giving me the runaround for months.

glossary.meaning: deliberately delay or avoid giving a clear answer

text: To put it bluntly, we can't afford another mistake.

glossary.meaning: to say it directly / without softening

text: I'm in no rush — take your time.
Off the top of my head, I'd say around fifty.

glossary.word: off the top of my head
glossary.meaning: without thinking carefully / as an estimate

text: I'd say it's worth giving them a second chance.
It crossed my mind, but I dismissed the idea pretty quickly.

glossary.meaning: I briefly thought about it

text: I'm not going to pretend I understand everything yet.
Don't get me wrong — I'm not blaming you.

glossary.meaning: don't misunderstand what I'm saying

text: I might be reading between the lines, but it sounds personal.

glossary.meaning: understand a hidden meaning / what isn't said directly

text: It depends on how strict they want to be about it.
I'd take that with a pinch of salt if I were you.

glossary.word: take it with a pinch of salt
glossary.meaning: don't fully believe it / be sceptical

text: We should really nip this in the bud before it gets worse.

glossary.meaning: stop it early before it grows into a problem

text: There's a thin line between being honest and being rude.

glossary.meaning: a small difference separating two things

text: I'll be the first to admit I was wrong about that.
Let's not beat around the bush — what do you actually want?

glossary.meaning: avoid the main point / talk indirectly

text: He's been bending over backwards to make things right.

glossary.meaning: make a great effort to help / please someone

text: You can't please everyone, no matter how hard you try.
I get where you're coming from, but I still have my doubts.
I'd take a rain check, if that's alright with you.

glossary.meaning: politely postpone an invitation to another time

text: Honestly, that sounds like more trouble than it's worth.

glossary.meaning: not worth the effort it requires

text: We're not seeing eye to eye on this, are we?

glossary.meaning: agree / share the same view

text: Let's call it a day and pick it up tomorrow.

glossary.meaning: stop working for now
resume / continue
